page: Microsoft Azure EC16as_cc_v5 is an x86_64 architecture server belonging to the ECACCV5 family. It features 16 dedicated vCPUs powered by an AMD EPYC 7763 processor with 8 physical cores and 2 threads per core, running at a clock speed of 3.525 GHz. The processor includes 32 KB of L1d cache, 0.5 MB of L2 cache, and 32 MB of L3 cache. It is equipped with 128.0 GB of system memory, providing a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core. This server does not include local storage or dedicated GPUs, though it is capable of utilizing Premium Storage.

Benchmark results indicate strong single-core CPU performance, as evidenced by stress-ng single-core metrics placing it in the top 25%. Multi-core CPU performance and PassMark CPU scores fall within the average range. Memory performance is mixed; while the PassMark memory score is strong, uncached large-block memory write bandwidth is weak, placing in the bottom 25%. Conversely, cached small-block read-only memory bandwidth remains average. Cryptographic operations are supported by hardware-accelerated AES, VAES, and SHA_NI instruction sets.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the high memory density of 8.0 GB per vCPU makes this server a viable option for memory-intensive workloads that do not require local NVMe storage or GPU acceleration. The balanced compute capabilities and high memory capacity make it suitable for relational databases, in-memory caching systems like Redis, enterprise applications, and moderate data processing tasks.
description: Microsoft Azure EC16as_cc_v5 is an x86_64 memory-optimized server featuring 16 dedicated vCPUs and 128.0 GB of RAM, powered by an AMD EPYC 7763 processor running at 3.525 GHz. This virtual machine lacks local storage and dedicated GPUs but is Premium Storage capable. Performance benchmarks reveal strong single-core CPU execution and high PassMark memory scores, though multi-core processing and uncached large-block memory write bandwidth are average to weak. Cryptographic tasks benefit from hardware-accelerated AES, VAES, and SHA_NI instructions. With an 8.0 GB per core memory allocation, this server provides a resource-dense profile without the added cost of local NVMe drives. It is suited for memory-intensive workloads such as relational databases, Redis caching, and enterprise application hosting.
og_description: Microsoft Azure EC16as_cc_v5 is an x86_64 memory-optimized server with 16 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs, 128 GB RAM, and strong single-core performance, designed for databases and memory-intensive enterprise workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure EC16as_cc_v5 is an x86_64 memory-optimized server with 16 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs, 128 GB RAM, and strong single-core performance.
tagline: A memory-optimized virtual server featuring dedicated AMD EPYC processors and high memory density for database and caching workloads.
bullet_points: 16 dedicated vCPUs powered by an AMD EPYC 7763 processor at 3.525 GHz
128.0 GB of system memory providing 8.0 GB per core
Strong single-core CPU and PassMark memory benchmark performance
No local storage or GPU, optimized for external Premium Storage
Suited for relational databases, Redis caching, and enterprise applications
